denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 01/31/2026
Cigna Open Access Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W101013632          Group #: GRP-1128
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-121557
Claim #: CLM-2026-194785
Date of Service (requested): 01/11/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Ridgeway Physician Services

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 01/13/2026 for Diagnostic imaging and cyst aspiration if indicated related to Fibrocystic breast pain, severe (N60.19). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Diagnostic imaging and cyst aspiration if indicated
Diagnosis (as submitted): Fibrocystic breast pain, severe (N60.19)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: Coverage Policy 108 under Cigna Medical Coverage Policy and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG)

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in Coverage Policy 108 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Cigna medical policy requires documented failure of prerequisite therapies before Diagnostic imaging and cyst aspiration if indicated. Submitted records do not establish failure of required lower-cost options for Fibrocystic breast pain, severe (N60.19).

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the MCG Care Guidelines (edition not specified) and MCG Care Guidelines (edition not specified) and MCG Care Guidelines (edition not specified) and utilization-management requirements associated with step therapy missing. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a Cigna medical director regarding this determination before or during the appeal process., they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W101013632) and reference UM-2026-121557. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
Medical Review Unit
clinical_context: The 27-year-old member has Fibrocystic breast pain, severe (N60.19). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Diagnostic imaging and cyst aspiration if indicated recommended as the next appropriate step. New focal pain; ultrasound recommended by PCP. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that step therapy missing bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
